fields.description:
## Why

Both link bugs were invisible in telemetry: the batch outcome reports mutation-level `error=None` and "applied N of N" but never the per-sub-op counts, so a link/comment/label that silently no-ops (or fails non-fatally, logged only to stderr) leaves the pass green. The inbound applier already returns `links_applied`; the outbound batch path does not. Make a silent no-op fail loudly — safely.

## What

- Surface `links_applied`, `comments_applied`, `labels_applied` in the **structured outbound batch outcome** (queryable, not only logged) and the `RECON:` line — parity with the inbound applier's existing `links_applied`.
- Add a **silent-no-op canary**: when a mutation carries N sub-ops of a kind but `applied == 0`, that is a failure condition (`computed > 0 && applied == 0`), surfaced in the outcome/telemetry and asserted by a test that would have caught the link drop.
- **Rollout safety:** the fail-loud invariant lands **warn-first** — emit telemetry + a `WARNING` behind a flag/canary — and is promoted to hard-fail only after a baseline pass that emits zero such warnings. Reverting to warn is a flag flip, no other code change. This avoids hard-failing production syncs on a false positive.
- Sequencing: depends on story D (the single consolidated applier) so the counts come from one path.
- Out of scope (justified): making the threshold itself configurable — the simple `computed > 0 && applied == 0` invariant IS the contract; only enforce-vs-warn is flagged. A configurable threshold is YAGNI.

## Scope

- In: outcome/telemetry plumbing in `batch_dispatch.py` / `applier.py` / `reconcile.py`; the canary test; the warn-first flag.
- Out: the probe wiring (story F); applier consolidation (story D).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The structured outbound batch outcome and the `RECON:` line surface `links_applied`, `comments_applied`, `labels_applied`.
- [ ] A test asserts that a mutation with computed sub-ops > 0 but applied == 0 fails (reproduces what the link drop would have triggered).
- [ ] The fail-loud invariant rolls out warn-first (telemetry + `WARNING`) behind a flag; promotion to hard-fail and reversion to warn are a flag flip with no other code change.
- [ ] Sync semantics are otherwise unchanged (telemetry + guard only); the full reconciler suite is green.
- [ ] The canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings (anti-change-detector).
